name: Au Sable River — Lower (Oscoda)

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up from Lake Huron.

patterns.title: Fall Au Sable salmon (Foote Dam)
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners
patterns.where: Below Foote Dam (Iosco County) downstream to Oscoda
patterns.details: The Lake Huron side of the Au Sable. Foote Dam is the limit of upstream salmon passage. Less crowded than Lake Michigan tribs.

notes: The Au Sable's lower 12 miles below Foote Dam is salmon + walleye water. Distinct from the Holy Water (upper Au Sable trout system).

name: South Holston River (below SoHo Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Premier trophy brown trout tailwater in the Southeast — possibly in the country. Heavy class fish are common, 24+ in regular.

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: Wild + stocked. Very strong population.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.size: Lower river, warm water below the trout zone.

patterns.title: Sulfur hatch dry-fly window
patterns.technique: Size 16-18 sulfur emerger + dun; CDC sulfur cripple; long-fine leaders 6X-7X
patterns.where: Throughout the 14-mi tailwater; classic riffle-to-pool transitions
patterns.details: The SoHo sulfur hatch is the signature dry-fly event in TN. Fish rise selectively — match emerger vs. dun vs. cripple. The brown trout get genuinely picky. Dam generation schedule matters — fish during wadeable low-flow windows.

patterns.title: Midge dry + emerger fishing
patterns.technique: Size 22-26 zebra midge nymph; size 24-26 midge dry (Griffith's gnat cluster); RS-2 emerger
patterns.where: Slow flat water + tailouts
patterns.details: When generation is OFF and water is clear, SoHo trout sip midges in unprecedented numbers. 7X tippet, perfect drift. The most technical small-fly fishery in TN.

patterns.title: Streamer fishing trophy browns
patterns.technique: Galloup's Sex Dungeon, Boogie Man, Sculpzilla; smaller patterns when water is low + clear
patterns.where: Deep banks + log jams + tailwater current seams
patterns.details: SoHo browns hit articulated meat hardest in cold low-light periods. Wading + drift-boat both work. 7-8 wt rod for the trophy class.

patterns.title: Scud + sowbug nymphing (year-round)
patterns.technique: Size 14-18 grey/pink scud; size 14-16 sowbug; under indicator
patterns.where: Throughout the tailwater on any moving water
patterns.details: Scuds + sowbugs are the year-round food forms in TN tailwaters. When you don't know what they're on, drift these. Pink scud is the SoHo specialty.

patterns.title: Dam generation timing
patterns.technique: Fish low-flow windows for wade fishing; high-flow windows for drift boats
patterns.where: TVA posts schedule daily at tva.com/lakes/Generation-Schedules
patterns.details: TVA generates South Holston Dam for hydropower. When generation is on, the river rises 3-4 ft + becomes float-only. Wade-fishing windows are critical — check schedule before driving. Generation timing also drives bug activity.

regulations: Slot limit on brown trout: protect 16-22 in. 7-fish daily creel. Catch-and-release zones in the trophy area.
managementProgram: TWRA wild brown trout management — slot limit
notes: The South Holston is THE Southern brown trout tailwater. 14 miles below SoHo Dam in Sullivan County. The brown trout slot limit (protecting 16-22 in fish) is the foundation of the trophy fishery.

name: Watauga River (below Wilbur Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Like the SoHo — trophy brown water with strict regs.

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: TWRA stocked.

patterns.title: Sulfur hatch (same as SoHo but slightly different timing)
patterns.technique: Size 16-18 sulfur emerger + dun
patterns.where: Throughout the tailwater
patterns.details: Watauga sulfur hatch is on the same schedule as the SoHo. Same technical match-the-hatch fishery.

patterns.title: Streamer browns + slot-protection
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers; smaller patterns than the SoHo in tight water
patterns.where: Below Wilbur Dam

patterns.title: Midge + scud nymphing
patterns.technique: Zebra midge + scud + sowbug under indicator
patterns.where: Throughout

regulations: Brown trout slot limit (protect 16-22 in). 7-fish creel.
notes: The Watauga is the SoHo's twin — same fishery character, same regs, similar fish. Less crowded but every bit as good.

name: Clinch River (below Norris Dam)

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: TWRA stocked + holdover wild fish.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Trophy class — Clinch is famous for very large browns when conditions align.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Lower river warm zone.

patterns.title: Midge nymphing (the Clinch signature)
patterns.technique: Size 20-26 zebra midge (black, red, olive); UV2-Pheasant tail tiny; under indicator with split-shot
patterns.where: Throughout the 13-mi tailwater
patterns.details: The Clinch is a midge-fishery. Generations limited periods of midge clouds. 7X tippet, indicator nymphing, multiple-flies rig with weight. The most technical small-fly TN tailwater.

patterns.title: Sulfur evening dry fly
patterns.technique: Size 16-18 sulfur emerger + dun
patterns.where: Throughout

patterns.title: Streamer big browns
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers; smaller patterns when low/clear
patterns.where: Deep banks + below structure
patterns.details: Clinch browns get truly large (30+ in documented). Pre-spawn fall fish are the prize.

patterns.title: Wooly Bugger for wading anglers
patterns.technique: Olive Wooly Bugger size 8-10 stripped or swung
patterns.where: Throughout — works for wading anglers without fly gear depth
patterns.details: When in doubt, throw an olive Wooly Bugger on the Clinch. Even very large trout will eat it.

regulations: Slot limit + 7-fish creel.
notes: Most-fished TN tailwater for its proximity to Knoxville. 13 miles of cold tailwater below Norris Dam.

name: Caney Fork River (below Center Hill Dam)

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: Heavily stocked TWRA + wild reproduction.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Trophy class — Caney browns are famous, especially in the upper tailwater zone.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Below the trout zone toward Carthage; warmwater character.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Migrate up from the Cumberland.

patterns.title: Year-round midge + scud nymphing
patterns.technique: Size 20-22 zebra midge; size 14-16 scud (pink, olive); under indicator
patterns.where: Throughout the 28-mi tailwater
patterns.details: Caney Fork is a year-round nymph fishery. The cold dam discharges keep trout active all season. Pink scud is a regional standout — TVA dam discharge color-shifts the natural scuds.

patterns.title: Caddis + sulfur dry-fly hatches
patterns.technique: Size 14-18 caddis (Elk Hair Caddis, X-Caddis); sulfur emerger + dun in late spring
patterns.where: Riffle-to-pool transitions in the upper tailwater

patterns.title: Streamer fishing trophy browns
patterns.technique: Galloup's Sex Dungeon, Boogie Man, Kreelex (flashabou attractor)
patterns.where: Deep banks below the dam; the trophy zone above Happy Hollow
patterns.details: Caney browns key off shad + sculpin forage. Kreelex (Chuck Kraft, regional) is the flashabou pattern that catches everything on this water.

patterns.title: Junk-fly drift for stocked rainbows
patterns.technique: Mop Fly, Squirmy Wormy, Y2K, San Juan Worm
patterns.where: Throughout — but especially the public-access points
patterns.details: TWRA stocks the Caney heavily through the year. Junk flies clean up on fresh stockers.

patterns.title: Center Hill Dam generation timing
patterns.technique: Watch the TVA generation schedule
patterns.where: tva.com/Energy/Our-Power-System/Hydroelectric/Center-Hill
patterns.details: Center Hill Dam runs unpredictably. Best wade-fishing windows are early morning before generation kicks in + late summer afternoons when load drops. Always check the schedule + listen for the dam siren.

regulations: Slot limit + 7-fish creel. Some catch-and-release zones above Happy Hollow.
notes: The Caney Fork is Middle Tennessee's flagship tailwater. 28 miles of cold water below Center Hill Dam. Drains to the Cumberland near Carthage.

name: Hiwassee River (below Apalachia Powerhouse)
signatureSpecies: Rainbow trout, brown trout, smallmouth bass

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: Stocked + reproducing.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Lower river + warm zone — unusual to have both trout + bass.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Spring run from Chickamauga Lake.

patterns.title: Yellow jacket bank-fishing summer dry
patterns.technique: Size 12-14 Yellow Jacket pattern (foam, yellow-and-black banded)
patterns.where: Bank-side wherever overhanging trees + flowers attract bees
patterns.details: The Hiwassee's yellow jacket bite is genuinely unique to this water. Bees fall off bankside flowers + bushes in midsummer afternoons. Fish rise to them like terrestrials. Bank-slap technique.

patterns.title: Streamer + dry-dropper through the rapids
patterns.technique: Articulated white streamer (fall/winter delayed harvest); dry-dropper with Stimulator + caddis dropper
patterns.where: The Hiwassee's class II-III rapids
patterns.details: The Hiwassee is unique among TN tailwaters — it has serious whitewater. Float by kayak or raft. Fall delayed-harvest is the most-celebrated window for big browns.

patterns.title: Smallmouth in the lower river
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, in-line spinners, surface poppers
patterns.where: Below the cold-water zone toward Reliance + Etowah

regulations: Standard trout regs. Delayed-harvest section October-March.
notes: The Hiwassee is a hybrid trout/smallmouth/whitewater river. Most-paddled trout water in the Southeast. The fall + winter delayed-harvest is a destination season.

name: Holston River (below Cherokee Dam)
signatureSpecies: Rainbow trout, brown trout, striped bass

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: TWRA stocked.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Migrate up from Cherokee Lake in summer for cool tailwater discharge. World-record class fishery for stripers in trout water.

patterns.title: Summer striped bass on flies
patterns.technique: Big white streamers (Lefty's Deceiver, Half and Half, Bob Popovics Hollow Fleye); 9-10 wt rod with sink tip
patterns.where: Cool tailwater pools below Cherokee Dam where stripers stage during summer thermal-refuge migration
patterns.details: World-class warmwater fly fishing — the trout-water stripers of the Holston are an East TN specialty. 25-40 lb fish hit articulated baitfish in 60-65°F water while everyone else is dredging deep lake holes.

patterns.title: Trout nymphing + dry-fly
patterns.technique: Midge nymphs + scud + sulfur dry-fly in season
patterns.where: Upper tailwater immediately below Cherokee Dam

regulations: Striped bass with TN reciprocity license + trout stamp where applicable.
notes: The Holston is the rare tailwater with both trout AND world-class striped bass — driven by thermal refuge migration from Cherokee Lake in summer.

name: Elk River (below Tims Ford Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Strong wild brown population — Elk browns are quality.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Lower river warm zone.

patterns.title: Sulfur dry + emerger
patterns.technique: Sulfur emerger + dun size 16-18
patterns.where: Throughout the tailwater

patterns.title: Wading scud + midge nymphing
patterns.technique: Size 14-16 scud + size 20-22 midge under indicator
patterns.where: Wadeable stretches below the dam

notes: Less-crowded Middle TN tailwater. Quality fishery for those willing to drive past Caney Fork. Tims Ford Dam discharge.

name: Obey River (below Dale Hollow Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: World-record class historically — Dale Hollow Lake holds the IGFA world-record smallmouth, and the Obey tailwater browns are part of the same cold-water system.

patterns.title: Streamer trophy browns
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers
patterns.where: Deep pools below Dale Hollow Dam

patterns.title: Sulfur evening hatch
patterns.technique: Sulfur emerger + dun
patterns.where: Throughout

notes: Possibly the most-isolated TN tailwater — 90+ mi from any major city. Trophy browns + low pressure.

name: Norris Lake
signatureSpecies: Striped bass, smallmouth bass, walleye, lake trout

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: World-class striper fishery — Norris produces TN's largest stripers.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Clear deep TVA reservoir = trophy smallmouth habitat.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Norris held the TN walleye record. Spring spawn run up the Powell + Clinch arms.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Stocked by TWRA — rare in the Southeast.

patterns.title: Summer striper schooling
patterns.technique: Live shad on planer boards + downlines; topwater pencil poppers at dawn; casting Jigging Rapalas into surface boils
patterns.where: Main lake basin off Big Creek + Cove Creek; the river channels
patterns.details: Norris stripers gorge on summer threadfin shad. Schools blow up on the surface at dawn + dusk. Charter scene is robust.

patterns.title: Spring + fall smallmouth on rocky points
patterns.technique: Float-n-fly with hair jigs; Ned rigs; suspending jerkbaits
patterns.where: Rocky points + bluffs throughout the lake; spring transitions
patterns.details: Norris smallmouth are clear-water finicky. Float-n-fly with 1/8 oz hair jig is the Tennessee method. Cold-water months are best.

patterns.title: Walleye spring spawn run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/4-3/8 oz; small swimbaits; trolling Reef Runners
patterns.where: Powell + Clinch river arms; pre-spawn staging
patterns.details: Norris is the rare TN reservoir with serious walleye. Spring run up the river arms is the destination.

notes: East TN's premier deep-clear reservoir. 34,200 acres + 295 ft max depth. The most multi-species reservoir in TN.

name: Cherokee Lake

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Strong striper fishery. Summer thermal-refuge migration to Holston tailwater.

patterns.title: Striper trolling + planer boards
patterns.technique: Live shad on planer boards; downlines in summer thermocline; trolling umbrella rigs in spring
patterns.where: Main lake + the Holston River arm
patterns.details: Summer stripers chase the Holston tailwater cool water — see the Holston River entry for the fly fishery they create.

patterns.title: Spring crappie spawn
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under bobber
patterns.where: Brush + dock pilings in spawning coves

notes: Cherokee + Douglas + Boone are the Holston-system reservoirs. Cherokee is the largest of the three.

name: Douglas Lake

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Strong largemouth fishery — host of major bass tournaments.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Spring run up the French Broad.

patterns.title: Spring white bass French Broad run
patterns.technique: Jigs + small swimbaits + in-line spinners
patterns.where: French Broad River arm above the lake
patterns.details: Massive spring schooling event. White bass push miles up the French Broad to spawn.

patterns.title: Spawn-season largemouth
patterns.technique: Senkos + lizards on beds; chatterbaits on pre-spawn flats
patterns.where: Flooded buckbrush + the upper-lake river channel arms

notes: Sevier County's big bass reservoir. Massive shoreline + heavy timber. Bass tournament destination.

name: Watauga Lake
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, walleye, lake trout, kokanee salmon

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Watauga Lake held the IGFA all-tackle walleye record at one point (25 lb in 1960).

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: One of the very few Southern lake-trout waters.

species.name: Kokanee salmon
species.notes: Stocked — rare Southern kokanee fishery.

patterns.title: Walleye deep summer trolling
patterns.technique: Lead-core or downrigger trolling; crawler harnesses; deep cranks
patterns.where: Main basin 30-80 ft
patterns.details: Watauga walleye orient to deep cool water in summer. The historical world record came from here.

patterns.title: Lake trout + kokanee deep jigging
patterns.technique: White tubes + small jigging spoons; jigging shad for kokanee
patterns.where: Deep basin 80-150 ft
patterns.details: One of very few Southern waters where you can target lake trout + kokanee. Unique fishery for TN.

managementProgram: TWRA walleye + lake trout + kokanee stocking
notes: High-elevation deep-clear reservoir. The closest thing to a Western lake in the South. Famous for the historical walleye world record.

name: South Holston Lake

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Stocked.

patterns.title: Float-n-fly smallmouth
patterns.technique: 1/8 oz hair jig under foam float; ultra-light line
patterns.where: Bluff banks + rocky points; cold-water suspended fish
patterns.details: The float-n-fly is a TN signature winter smallmouth technique. SoHo Lake is the home water for this method.

patterns.title: Walleye spring spawn run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow
patterns.where: Watauga River arm pre-spawn

notes: Sits above South Holston Dam — discharges feed the famous SoHo tailwater downstream.

name: Boone Lake
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, smallmouth bass, crappie, walleye

patterns.title: Mixed bag spring crappie + bass
patterns.technique: Crappie jigs; spinnerbaits + plastic worms for bass
patterns.where: Coves + brush across the lake

notes: Tri-cities reservoir (Bristol / Johnson City / Kingsport). Multi-species generalist fishery.

name: Fort Loudoun Lake

patterns.title: Striper trolling downtown Knoxville
patterns.technique: Trolling planer boards; downlines in summer
patterns.where: Main channel through Knoxville

notes: The Knoxville-metro reservoir. Tennessee River through downtown.

name: Tellico Lake
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, walleye, crappie, striped bass

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: TWRA stocked — strong populations.

patterns.title: Walleye on rocky points
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + jig + minnow
patterns.where: Main-lake points + river channel edges

notes: Adjacent to Fort Loudoun (they share a canal). Strong multi-species fishery.

name: Watts Bar Lake

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Fall/winter run up Clinch + Tennessee arms.

patterns.title: Striper trolling main channel
patterns.technique: Planer boards + downlines
patterns.where: Main Tennessee River channel

patterns.title: Sauger fall + winter
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow on current breaks
patterns.where: Below the Clinch + Emory confluences

notes: Confluence of the Tennessee + Clinch + Emory. Massive 39,000-acre reservoir.

name: Chickamauga Lake
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, striped bass, crappie, sauger

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Chickamauga produces the largest largemouths in TN — multiple state records. Florida-strain stocking + grass.

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Winter run.

patterns.title: Grass-mat largemouth (Chickamauga signature)
patterns.technique: Frog fishing over hydrilla + milfoil; punching heavy weights; chatterbaits on edges
patterns.where: Hydrilla flats throughout the lake — Sale Creek, Soddy-Daisy, Possum Creek
patterns.details: Chickamauga is THE Florida-strain largemouth destination in TN. The hydrilla beds produce 8-12 lb fish regularly. Bassmaster Elite + MLF tours visit. Frog bite is legendary.

patterns.title: Winter sauger run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow on current breaks
patterns.where: Below the Watts Bar Dam tailwater area

regulations: Largemouth slot limit 15-18 in (varies — confirm current TWRA regs).
notes: TN's premier trophy largemouth water. Two new TN state records have come from here in the past decade.

name: Nickajack Lake
signatureSpecies: Striped bass, largemouth bass, sauger, crappie

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Below Chickamauga Dam tailwater.

patterns.title: Chickamauga Dam tailwater sauger
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow vertically
patterns.where: Immediately below Chickamauga Dam in the Nickajack headwaters

notes: Smaller link in the Tennessee River chain. Tailwater sauger fishery below Chickamauga Dam is the signature event.

name: Tims Ford Lake

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: One of the best smallmouth waters in Middle TN.

patterns.title: Float-n-fly smallmouth
patterns.technique: 1/8 oz hair jig under foam float
patterns.where: Bluff banks + rocky points

notes: Feeds the Elk River tailwater. Clear deep reservoir, good multi-species.

name: Center Hill Lake

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: World-class — Center Hill stripers run with the largest in the South.

patterns.technique: Live shad downlines + planer boards; topwater pencils at dawn
patterns.where: Main lake basin off Cookeville + Sligo

patterns.title: Float-n-fly smallmouth
patterns.technique: 1/8 oz hair jig under foam float
patterns.where: Bluff banks

notes: Sits above Center Hill Dam — discharges feed the Caney Fork tailwater. Premier striper + smallmouth combo.

name: Dale Hollow Lake

species.notes: Dale Hollow holds the IGFA all-tackle world record smallmouth bass (11 lb 15 oz, 1955). Still produces 6+ lb fish.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: One of the rare Southern lake trout fisheries.

patterns.where: Bluff walls + rocky points throughout the lake
patterns.details: Dale Hollow is the SMALLMOUTH lake — David Hayes' 11 lb 15 oz fish from 1955 still stands as the IGFA all-tackle record. Quality smallmouth fishing year-round.

notes: The smallmouth lake of America. Spans TN + KY. Clear deep TVA reservoir + native bass forage = world-class fish.

name: Old Hickory Lake
signatureSpecies: Striped bass, largemouth bass, crappie, white bass

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Below the dam in fall/winter.

patterns.where: Main Cumberland River channel

patterns.title: Spring white bass run
patterns.technique: Jigs + spinners
patterns.where: Upper-lake tributary arms

notes: Nashville-metro reservoir on the Cumberland River. Most-fished by middle TN urban anglers.

name: J. Percy Priest Lake

species.notes: Percy Priest is the premier hybrid striper water in TN.

patterns.where: Main lake basin off Hamilton Creek + Stewarts Ferry
patterns.details: Percy Priest is the closest big-water reservoir to Nashville. Hybrid stripers school heavily in summer — birds working bait surface action.

name: Cordell Hull Lake

patterns.where: Main-lake points + bluff edges

name: Kentucky Lake (TN portion)

species.name: Crappie
species.notes: Kentucky Lake is the BEST crappie water in America by most measures. Tournaments + guide industry built around it.

species.name: Blue catfish
species.notes: World-class trophy blue cat fishery. Multiple state records.

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Winter run.

patterns.title: Spring crappie spawn
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under bobber; long-pole spider rigging
patterns.where: Brush + flooded timber in spawning coves; the Big Sandy + Eagle Creek arms
patterns.details: Kentucky Lake crappie spawn is one of the most-fished events in American freshwater. Spider-rigging with 8 rods is the local method. Limits of 1+ lb fish are routine.

patterns.title: Trophy blue catfish

patterns.where: Main river channel; below Kentucky Dam
patterns.details: Kentucky Lake produces 80+ lb blue cats every year. Cut bait on heavy gear. Charter scene is robust.

notes: America's premier crappie water. 110,000 acres on the TN side (the lake is huge, spanning TN + KY). Crappie + catfish + bass — everything.

name: Pickwick Lake (TN portion)

species.notes: One of the best trophy smallmouth waters in the South.

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Winter run.

name: Little River (Smokies)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Wild population — bigger fish are downstream.

species.name: Brook trout

species.name: Smallmouth bass

patterns.technique: Size 6 Hex dries
patterns.where: Silt-bottom holes

notes: The "below Mio" stretch is technically still the Au Sable mainstem. Series of small dams (Mio, Alcona, Loud, Five Channels, Cooke, Foote) — wider + slower than the Holy Water.

name: Grand River — Upper (Lansing + above)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Below Lansing dams.

patterns.title: Lansing-stretch smallmouth + carp
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned, in-line spinners; carp flies
patterns.where: Through downtown Lansing + East Lansing
patterns.details: Underrated urban smallmouth + carp through MSU's home city. Multiple low-head dams create habitat zones.

notes: Upper Grand. Lansing's urban smallmouth + carp fishery. Distinct from the salmon-zone Grand below Grand Rapids.

name: Lake Lansing

patterns.title: Suburban largemouth + bluegill
patterns.technique: Senkos for bass; wax worms for bluegill
patterns.where: Pad edges + dock corners

notes: East Lansing's home lake. MSU students fish here.

name: Reeds Lake

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR-stocked.

patterns.title: Deep-clear smallmouth
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned
patterns.where: Deep humps + bluff edges

notes: East Grand Rapids' home lake. Small + deep + clear. Premier suburban GR fishery.
